question: What do the men do with the tent in the clip right after "thank God we got this tent. Pouring out," is said?
answer: The tent pole leans to the side, almost about to fall over, so the man in jeans runs to go catch it.
correctOptionText: The tent pole tilts to the side, almost falling over, so the man in jeans runs over to catch and steady it.

options.text: They decide the tent is too unstable, take it back down completely, and move everyone into the house instead.

They realize the tent is missing parts, return it to the party store, and exchange it for a different canopy.
They stand under the newly raised canopy, remarking that it’s nice and dry beneath the tent even though the rain and water are roaring nearby.

question: What is the purpose of the man showing different plugs and explaining them in the video?
answer: To let travelers know that continental Europe uses different plugs than the United States and that an adapter/converter will be needed.
correctOptionText: To inform travelers that continental Europe uses different plugs than the United States and that an adapter/converter is needed.

options.text: To show that hotels in Europe have very few available outlets, so travelers should bring power strips to plug in more devices.
To advertise a specific brand of travel adapter that automatically adjusts voltage in any country without user input.

To visually compare the shapes of USA and Continental Europe plugs so viewers can recognize them at a glance when booking hotels.
To demonstrate how to safely rewire a US plug so it can fit directly into a European wall socket without using an adapter.
To teach viewers how to identify counterfeit electrical plugs being sold in street markets across Europe.
To inform travelers that continental Europe uses different plugs than the United States and that an adapter/converter is needed.
To warn that plugging a US-style device directly into a European socket without the right converter can damage the device.
To show that European trains and buses always provide both US and European outlets for travelers’ convenience.
To compare UK/Malta plugs with European wall sockets and highlight that high‑draw appliances like hair dryers may not be safe to use.

question: What visual cues show that the man was upset after the announcer says, “Dempsey missed it incredibly”?
answer: The man grabs the net and bends over after missing, and then jogs out, looks at the ground, and covers his head in his hands, then covers his mouth.
correctOptionText: He grabs the net and bends over after missing, then jogs away while looking down at the ground, puts his head in his hands, and finally covers his mouth.

options.text: He lies on the turf in the box as defenders scramble, while a teammate stands with his hands on his head in shock.
